nativeRange.habitat: Inshore coral reef walls and drop-offs, typically near caves, ledges and overhangs at 15–70 m depth.
nativeRange.biotope: Deeper reef ledges and cave entrances

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and overhangs

diet.notes: Planktivorous in the wild; generally takes frozen mysis, enriched brine, copepods and amphipods in captivity, and over time tends to accept high-quality pellets and flakes. Multiple small feedings per day are commonly advised, and a refugium seeded with pods is often recommended.

summary: The Sunburst Anthias (Serranocirrhitus latus) is a shy, deep-reef species generally kept singly in a well-decorated reef tank with caves and subdued lighting. It is reef-safe and tends to accept frozen foods once settled.
careNotes: The Sunburst Anthias is also sold under the names 'Fathead Anthias' and 'Fathead Sunburst Anthias', both of which refer to the same species, Serranocirrhitus latus. The 'fathead' label simply reflects the species' characteristically deep, blunt-headed body profile. Unlike many anthias that form large, active schools near the reef crest, the Sunburst Anthias tends to stay low, hugging cave entrances and overhanging ledges at depths where light is reduced. In the aquarium it is most commonly kept as a single specimen; pairs or small harems can work in larger tanks, but only one male should be present per group, and any group is generally advised to be introduced at the same time to reduce dominance disputes. Like other anthias, this species is a protogynous hermaphrodite, meaning the dominant female in a group will typically transition to male if the existing male is lost. It appreciates a tank with plenty of live rock arranged to provide caves, overhangs, and shaded retreats, and tends to do best when the lower portions of the aquarium are not blasted with intense lighting.
breedingNotes: The Sunburst Anthias is a protogynous hermaphrodite that forms loose harems on wild reefs, with the dominant female transitioning to male when needed. Captive breeding is very rarely reported, and virtually all specimens in the trade are wild-collected.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Anthiadidae, Perciformes), max size (13.0 cm), native range (Western Pacific: Moluccas to Fiji, north to Izu Islands and Taiwan, south to New Caledonia and Great Barrier Reef; recently Tonga), depth (15–70 m), and temperature (22–27 °C). Designated secondary Reef2Reef wiki returned 403; swapped to FishBase.

additional.name: ReefBay care guide (via web search)
additional.notes: Cross-checked minimum tank size (75 US gal, vs. LiveAquaria's 70 US gal), peaceful/shy temperament, reef-safe, feeding regime (multiple small feedings; needs variety), and behavior (bottom-dwelling, prefers live rock and subdued light, single male per tank).

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding is rarely reported; sourcing is virtually all wild-collected.
